description: If the user doesn't provide a detail the agent asked for, the agent should move on and not keep asking for it.

conversation.text: Every Monday at 9am, post 'Have a great week, team!' to Slack.
Which Slack channel should I post to?

[The user has no channel and won't provide one. Every time the agent asks for the channel — whether as a question or in a setup card, even if it asks more than once — skip or dismiss it without giving a channel, and never pick or invent one.]

processExpectations: The agent asked for the user to provide a Slack channel, rather than silently picking one itself.
The user did not provide the channel, and then the agent moved on — leaving it blank or for setup — never asking for the channel again.

executionScenarios.name: channel-skipped
executionScenarios.description: Built on a Monday schedule posting to Slack; the user didn't give a channel, so it's left for setup
executionScenarios.dataSetup: No mock data is needed. If the Slack node's channel is empty, the workflow fails its validation check before any node runs, so no API call is made.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow is built correctly: a Monday 9am schedule trigger feeding a Slack message node with the text 'Have a great week, team!'. If the Slack channel is empty, the workflow fails its validation check ('Not a valid Slack Channel ID or name') — treat that as fine (an empty channel is something to set up later, not a build mistake) and a pass. If a channel is set and the run succeeds, that's also a pass. Only judge the workflow's structure and how it handles the channel when it runs; don't judge anything about the conversation.
